paper_id: Goncalves et al. - 2007 - An electrocardiogram (ECG) domain ontology
input_limitations: The markdown extraction omits figures as [FIGURE] and contains some encoding/formula artifacts, but the main prose sections are readable.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology purpose is to bring in a theory of the electrocardiogram (ECG).

Considering  that  the  main  goal  of  this  ontology  is  to  bring  in  an  electrocardiogram theory which is independent of specific applications, the competency questions we have defined reflect this intent.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly frame the ontology as providing a domain theory of ECG, which maps directly to supporting domain understanding and conceptualization.

evidence_quotes.quote: This initiative then  addresses  the  need  for  semantic  interoperability,  in  general,  and  data integration, in particular, in Health Informatics.
Besides, it also provides an approach for developing ECG automatic analysis systems.

We advocate that the theory of the ECG conveyed in this paper provides  (i)  a  knowledge  repository  about  this  universe  of  discourse;  (ii)  a  reference conceptual model that may be used for promoting interoperability in Health Informatics; and (iii) a usefulness formal model for ECG analysis systems.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly states intended uses for interoperability, data integration, and developing ECG analysis systems, which match the Intervening rule for integration and system-development purposes.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology  purpose  is  to  provide  a  theory  of  the  ECG  in  order  to:  (i)  address  semantic interoperability  and  data/standard  integration  between  health  systems,  which  is  the currently key point of the research on Health Informatics [Ackerman et al. 2002]; (ii) allow  a  novel  artificial  intelligence  approach  for  ECG  automatic  analysis;  and  (iii) convey a knowledge repository about ECG.

We advocate that the theory of the ECG conveyed in this paper provides  (i)  a  knowledge  repository  about  this  universe  of  discourse;  (ii)  a  reference conceptual model that may be used for promoting interoperability in Health Informatics; and (iii) a usefulness formal model for ECG analysis systems.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly described as a knowledge repository and reference conceptual model, which fits asynchronous documentation/reference use.

evidence_quotes.quote: - CQ9. What  kind  of  information  does  a  physician  use  to  identify  variations  in  the morphology and timing of events in the ECG for inferring an interpretation?
This information is paramount to the physicians' analysis.

uncertain_goals.reason: The text hints at diagnostic interpretation and physician analysis, but the ontology is framed mainly as a domain theory and basis for analysis systems rather than as a decision-making model itself.
